skill.name: create-plan
skill.description: Creates concise, read-only implementation plans for coding tasks, including scope, ordered action items, validation, risks, and open questions.
skill.summary: Create structured coding plans before implementation work begins.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are false positives: SKILL.md line 19 uses Markdown backticks for file and directory names, and lines 42-63 show a Markdown plan template. Static weak cryptography findings are also false positives from ordinary words such as description, short-description, and describing on lines 3, 5, and 29. No prompt injection, code execution, network access, credential access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Coding requests can become vague when scope, risks, and validation are not defined early. This skill turns a coding request into a concise plan with scope, ordered steps, tests, and open questions.
actual_capabilities: Creates a final plan when the user explicitly asks for one.
Keeps the planning workflow read-only and avoids file changes.
Scans project documentation and relevant files before planning.
Separates in-scope work from out-of-scope work.
Builds ordered checklist items from discovery through validation.
Adds limited open questions only when they are blocking.
limitations: Does not implement code changes or update files.
Does not replace detailed architecture review for complex systems.
Depends on available repository context for accurate planning.
May make reasonable assumptions when information is missing.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Turn a feature request into clear implementation steps with likely files, tests, and rollout notes.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Refactor
use_cases.description: Outline a focused refactor plan that separates discovery, code changes, validation, and risk checks.

use_cases.title: Clarify a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Create a concise investigation and fix plan before touching production code.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a small coding task needs structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a plan for adding this small coding change. Include scope, ordered action items, validation, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Implementation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when planning a new feature across several files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a plan for implementing this feature. Read the relevant context first, then include likely files, tests, risks, and rollout considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Planning Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing a behavior-preserving refactor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a read-only refactor plan. Identify the current structure, proposed changes, validation steps, edge cases, and any blocking questions.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Delivery Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when coordinating a larger change with uncertainty.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an advanced implementation plan for this coding initiative. Separate in-scope and out-of-scope work, sequence discovery through rollout, and call out assumptions.

output_examples.input: Create a plan to add password reset emails to this app.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with scope, ordered discovery and implementation steps, validation for email delivery, edge cases, and open questions about providers.

output_examples.input: Create a plan for refactoring the billing service.
output_examples.output: A concise refactor plan that identifies likely modules, preserves current behavior, adds targeted tests, and includes rollback considerations.

output_examples.input: Create a plan to fix intermittent search failures.
output_examples.output: An investigation-first plan covering reproduction, logging review, targeted fixes, regression tests, and risk checks for search edge cases.

best_practices: Provide the coding goal, repository area, and any known constraints.
Ask for a plan before implementation starts.
Review open questions before using the plan for execution.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when you want code edited immediately.
Do not expect exhaustive architecture analysis from a small task prompt.
Do not skip validation steps when turning the plan into work.

faq.question: Does this skill change files?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly operates in read-only mode and returns a plan only.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when you want a clear implementation plan before coding starts.

faq.question: Can it ask follow-up questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks one or two questions only when they are blocking.

faq.question: What does the plan include?
faq.answer: It includes intent, scope, ordered action items, validation steps, risks, and optional open questions.

faq.question: Is it specific to one programming language?
faq.answer: No. It adapts to the language, framework, and project structure it can inspect.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill declares support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
